name: NUST LMS API
description:
NUST Learning Management System API for developer integrations (Moodle AJAX wrapper).
Authenticate with your NUST LMS credentials via `POST /auth/login` to receive a bearer token, then call any operation with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. The NustDevKit gateway logs into the LMS on your behalf and manages the underlying session (cookie + sesskey) server-side, so you never handle the ephemeral sesskey.

folders.name: Authentication
folders.description: Exchange NUST LMS credentials for a gateway bearer token

folders.name: Calendar
folders.description: Calendar operations. On NUST LMS, these "action events" are how deadlines are surfaced — assignment, lab, quiz, and other submission due dates each appear as a calendar event on the day the work must be submitted.

requests.name: login
requests.description: Authenticates against NUST LMS on your behalf and returns a gateway JWT. The LMS session (cookie + sesskey) is created and held server-side; use the returned token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` for every other operation.

requests.name: getCoreRecentCourses
requests.description:
Returns the courses the authenticated user has accessed most recently, ordered by last access time.
Moodle method: `core_course_get_recent_courses`

responses.name: Response_200
responses.status: List of recently accessed courses ordered by last access.

responseCode.detail: List of recently accessed courses ordered by last access.

requests.name: getEnrolledCoursesByTimeline
requests.description:
Returns all courses the user is enrolled in, filtered by timeline classification and sorted by a chosen field. Supports pagination via offset.
Moodle method: `core_course_get_enrolled_courses_by_timeline_classification`

responses.name: Response_200

requests.name: getCalendarEventsByTimesort
requests.description:
Returns action events (deadlines) across all enrolled courses, ordered by their sort timestamp. On NUST LMS these are submission due dates for assignments, labs, quizzes, and similar dated activities. Use `timesortfrom` to filter to upcoming deadlines only.
Moodle method: `core_calendar_get_action_events_by_timesort`

responses.name: Response_200
responses.text:

requests.name: getCalendarEventsByCourse
requests.description:
Returns all action events (deadlines) for a single course — assignment, lab, and quiz submission due dates, and other dated activities.
Moodle method: `core_calendar_get_action_events_by_course`

responses.name: Response_200
responses.status: Calendar events for the specified course.

responseCode.detail: Calendar events for the specified course.

requests.name: getUsersByField
requests.description:
Retrieves one or more user profiles by matching a specific profile field. Commonly used to look up the authenticated user's own profile by their ID.
Moodle method: `core_user_get_users_by_field`

queryParams.description: User profile field to match against when searching for users.
List of field values to look up. All values must be provided as strings even when the field is numeric (e.g., "123456" for an integer ID).

requests.name: getUserPreferences
requests.description:
Returns all stored preference key/value pairs for the specified user. Optionally filter to a single preference by name.
Moodle method: `core_user_get_user_preferences`

responses.name: Response_200
responses.status: User preferences list and optional warnings.

responseCode.detail: User preferences list and optional warnings.

queryParams.description: ID of the user whose preferences to retrieve.
Specific preference name to retrieve. Omit to retrieve all preferences.

requests.name: fetchNotifications
requests.description:
Returns pending site-level notifications for the given Moodle context. Returns an empty array when there are no pending notifications.
Moodle method: `core_fetch_notifications`

responses.name: Response_200
responses.status: Array of site notifications (empty array when none are pending).

responseCode.detail: Array of site notifications (empty array when none are pending).

queryParams.description: Moodle context ID for which to fetch notifications. The user context ID can be found in the user profile image URL path segment.

requests.name: getPopupNotifications
requests.description:
Returns the most recent popup notifications sent to a user, with support for pagination. Also returns the total count of unread notifications.
Moodle method: `message_popup_get_popup_notifications`

responses.name: Response_200
responses.status: Popup notifications and total unread count.

responseCode.detail: Popup notifications and total unread count.

responses.text:
